name: pyicat-plus
documentation:
# PyIcat-Plus

A python client for ICAT+.

## Documentation

https://pyicat-plus.readthedocs.io/

classes.name: pyicat_plus.client.messaging.IcatMessagingClient

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.messaging.IcatMessagingClient.check_health
documentation:
Raises an exception when:
- not connected
- no message consumers

Client for the ICAT message broker.

The message broker is currently ActiveMQ, a message
broker using the STOMP protocol. It also has a REST
server for monitoring the broker status.

functions.name: pyicat_plus.client.elogbook._message_category_and_type
documentation:
Derive the ICAT message category from the API message type.

The ICAT message types are:

- "annotation" (default for comments): editable and unformatted message
- "notification" (default for non-comments): formatted and not editable

Only comments can be editable and a message cannot be editable and
formatted at the same time.

documentation:
The payload tags can be eithers a list of strings or a list of dictionaries.
The return value are the merged tags as a list of dictionaries.

Client for the e-logbook part of the ICAT+ REST API.

REST API docs:
https://icatplus.esrf.fr/api-docs/

The ICAT+ server project:
https://gitlab.esrf.fr/icat/icat-plus/-/blob/master/README.md

classes.name: pyicat_plus.client.main.IcatClient

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.main.IcatClient.__init__
documentation:
---
param add_files_queue: Queue to be used when sending a message to `add_files_urls`.
param add_files_queue_monitor_port: REST server port to be used for monitor the `add_files_urls`
  ActiveMQ message brokers (same host as the message broker).
param add_files_urls: URLs of the ActiveMQ message brokers to be used for updating
  the file count of ICAT datasets.
param archive_queue: Queue to be used when sending a message to `archive_urls`.
param archive_queue_monitor_port: REST server port to be used for monitor the `archive_urls`
  ActiveMQ message brokers (same host as the message broker).
param archive_urls: URLs of the ActiveMQ message brokers to be used for updating the
  archival status of ICAT datasets.
param beamline: Default beamline to be used as metadata when sending messages to `metadata_urls`
  or `elogbook_url`.
param elogbook_metadata: Default electronic logbook metadata to be used when sending
  messages to `elogbook_url`.
param elogbook_timeout: POST timeout for `elogbook_url`.
param elogbook_token: Access token for restricted requests to `elogbook_url`.
param elogbook_url: URL of the ICAT+ REST server to be used for sending text or images
  to the electronic logbook and get information about investigations.
param feedback_timeout: GET timeout for `elogbook_url`.
param metadata_queue: Queue to be used when sending a message to `metadata_urls`.
param metadata_queue_monitor_port: REST server port to be used for monitor the `metadata_urls`
  ActiveMQ message brokers (same host as the message broker).
param metadata_urls: URLs of the ActiveMQ message brokers to be used for creating
  ICAT datasets from a directory with metadata.
param proposal: Default proposal to be used as metadata when sending messages to `metadata_urls`
  or `elogbook_url`.
param queue_timeout: Connection timeout for `metadata_urls`.
param update_metadata_queue: Queue to be used when sending a message to `update_metadata_urls`.
param update_metadata_queue_monitor_port: REST server port to be used for monitor
  the `update_metadata_urls` ActiveMQ message brokers (same host as the message broker).
param update_metadata_urls: URLs of the ActiveMQ message brokers to be used for update
  metadata of ICAT datasets.
---

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.main.IcatClient.store_processed_data
documentation: The 'raw' argument is shorthand for `metadata = {'input_datasets': ...}`.

Client object that provides access to these services:

- ActiveMQ message broker for creating datasets in ICAT
- ActiveMQ message broker for updating dataset metadata in ICAT
- ActiveMQ message broker for updating dataset file count in ICAT
- ActiveMQ message broker for updating dataset archiving status in ICAT
- RESTful interface for sending electronic logbook messages/images and get information about investigations

The RESTful interface is referred to as ICAT+ and the ActiveMQ message brokers are consumed by the "ingesters".

functions.name: pyicat_plus.client.investigation._select_investigation
documentation:
When `date` is not provided we take it to be "now".

This method returns the last investigation that contains
the date or has a start/end closest to the date. The
investigations are ordered from first to last created.

Optionally all open-ended investigations can be ignored.
Open-ended investigations have a start date but no end date.
These investigations are created by sending dataset or
investigation messages with start dates 48h outside any
official investigation.

```
param investigations

unsorted list of investigations

param date

possibly not fully deterministic date

param allow_open_ended

validation option

returns

matching investigation according to the selection rules
```

functions.name: pyicat_plus.client.investigation._filter_valid_investigations
documentation:
Filter out investigations with invalid time slots.

```
param investigations

unsorted list of investigations

param allow_open_ended

validation option

returns

list of valid investigations (preserve order)
```

functions.name: pyicat_plus.client.investigation._deterministic_date
documentation:
The date might

- not exist: take now
- have no time zone: add local timezone
- be a day without time: select time from the investigations or the default 8 a.m.

The resulting date is fully deterministic.

```
param date

possibly not fully deterministic date

param investigations

sorted by order of importance

returns

fully deterministic date
```

functions.name: pyicat_plus.client.investigation._select_investigation_from_sorted_list
documentation:
---
param date: point in time for which we want to find the corresponding investigation
param investigations: sorted by order of importance
returns: matching investigation according to the selection rules
---

classes.name: pyicat_plus.client.investigation.IcatInvestigationClient

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.investigation.IcatInvestigationClient._get_with_response_parsing
documentation:
Return `None` means the information is not available at this moment.
An empty list means that an error has occured on the server side or an
actual empty list is returned.

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.investigation.IcatInvestigationClient.investigation_info
documentation:
An investigation is defined by a time slot. Find an investigation (if any)
for a beamline, proposal and date ("now" when not provided). When there is
more than one investigation, select the closest one started before or at the date.
If there is no such investigation, get the closest investigation which starts after the date.

Client for the investigation part of the ICAT+ REST API.

An "investigation" is a time slot assigned to a particular proposal
at a particular beamline.

REST API docs:
https://icatplus.esrf.fr/api-docs/

The ICAT+ server project:
https://gitlab.esrf.fr/icat/icat-plus/-/blob/master/README.md

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.interface.Dataset.from_dict
documentation: Factory method to create a Dataset instance from a dictionary.

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.interface.IcatClientInterface.send_message
documentation:
Send a message to the proposal or beamline e-logbook.

```
param msg

The message content.

param msg_type

{'comment', 'debug', 'info', 'error', 'commandLine'}, optional.

param beamline

The beamline name of the proposal or beamline e-logbook.

param proposal

The proposal name of the e-logbook. Ignored if  is True.

param dataset

The specific dataset name to link the message to.

param beamline_only

if , the message will be stored in the beamline e-logbook.

param editable

Used with the  field, to determine the category of message. Annotation characterizes editable and unformatted messages, while Notification charaterizes non-editable and formatted messages.

param formatted

Used with the  field, to determine the category of message. Annotation characterizes editable and unformatted messages, while Notification charaterizes non-editable and formatted messages.

param mimetype

{'text/plain', 'text/html'}, optional.

param payload

Additional payload for the message. It can contain tags (list of strings or list of dictionaries), the machine, the software.
```

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.interface.IcatClientInterface.send_binary_data
documentation:
Send an image in base64 format to the proposal or beamline e-logbook.

```
param data

The binary message content.

param mimetype

{'text/plain', 'text/html'}, optional.

param beamline

The beamline name of the proposal or beamline e-logbook.

param proposal

The proposal name of the e-logbook. Ignored if  is True.

param beamline_only

if , the message will be stored in the beamline e-logbook.

param payload

Additional payload for the e-logbook message. It can contain tags (list of strings or list of dictionaries), the machine, the software.
```

```
param proposal

The proposal name of the e-logbook. Ignored if  is True.

param beamline_only

if , the message will be stored in the beamline e-logbook.

param payload

Additional payload for the e-logbook message. It can contain tags (list of strings or list of dictionaries), the machine, the software.
```

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.interface.IcatClientInterface.start_investigation
documentation:
Send a message to ActiveMQ to either synchronize the experiment session from the User Portal in ICAT or to create a test experiment session in ICAT.

```
param beamline

The beamline name of the proposal.

param proposal

The proposal name.

param start_datetime

The start date of the experiment session, timezone local time. Current date time by default.

param end_datetime

The end date of the experiment session, timezone local time.
```

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.interface.IcatClientInterface.store_dataset
documentation:
Request icat to store raw dataset.

:param beamline str: beamline name like id01, id15a, bm18...
:param proposal str: proposal name like in1169, blc14795, ihma429...
:param str dataset: dataset name.
:param str path: path to the raw dataset to store. Must be a folder.
:param dict metadata: metadata to associate to the dataset. Must contains keys defined by the appropriate application definition from https://gitlab.esrf.fr/icat/hdf5-master-config/-/blob/88a975039694d5dba60e240b7bf46c22d34065a0/hdf5_cfg.xml.
:param str store_filename: xml file with metadata to be stored.

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.interface.IcatClientInterface.store_processed_data
documentation:
Request icat to store a processed dataset.

:param beamline str: beamline name like id01, id15a, bm18...
:param proposal str: proposal name like in1169, blc14795, ihma429...
:param str dataset: dataset name like sample_XYZ.
:param str path: path to the processed dataset to store. Can be a file or a folder.
:param dict metadata: metadata to associate to the dataset. Must contains keys defined by the appropriate application definition from https://gitlab.esrf.fr/icat/hdf5-master-config/-/blob/88a975039694d5dba60e240b7bf46c22d34065a0/hdf5_cfg.xml.
:param tuple raw: Path to the raw dataset(s). Expects to be path to 'bliss dataset' folder(s). See https://confluence.esrf.fr/display/BM02KW/File+structure for
                  If processing rely on more than one dataset then all dataset folders must be provided.
:param str store_filename: xml file with metadata to be stored.

methods.name: pyicat_plus.client.interface.IcatClientInterface.store_dataset_from_file
documentation:
Send a message to ActiveMQ to store a dataset and the associated metadata from a xml file stored on the disk.

```
param store_filename

The XML filename containing all dataset metadata.
```
